evals.prompt: I'm on Kamal 1.8 and want to move my app to Kamal 2.0. Can you walk me through the whole upgrade?
evals.expected_output: Should suggest reading the existing config/deploy.yml, .env/.env.rb, and .kamal/secrets first. Should advise upgrading to Kamal 1.9.x first (gem install kamal --version 1.9.0) because 1.9 supports downgrading, and confirming a deploy works on 1.9. Should then cover upgrading the gem to Kamal 2 (gem install kamal), converting configuration (traefik block no longer valid -> proxy, healthcheck section removed, builder arch now required, app_port default changed from 3000 to 80), validating with kamal config, moving secrets from .env to .kamal/secrets, verifying the container port, and finally running kamal upgrade. Should warn to test in a non-production environment first.
assertions: Recommends reading the user's existing config/deploy.yml and secrets before asking questions
Advises upgrading to Kamal 1.9.x first because it supports downgrading
Mentions validating the converted config with kamal config
Notes the traefik block is no longer valid and is replaced by proxy
Notes the default app port changed from 3000 to 80
Ends with running kamal upgrade
Warns to test in a non-production environment first

evals.prompt: my traefik config stopped working after I bumped kamal to 2. what gives?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that in Kamal 2 the traefik configuration is no longer valid and is replaced by kamal-proxy, configured under the proxy block. Should mention the app_port default changed from 3000 to 80 (kamal-proxy assumes Thruster on port 80) and to set app_port if the app listens elsewhere. Should mention the healthcheck section was removed and proxy-role healthchecks now live under proxy/healthcheck. Should note that custom Traefik labels/args may or may not be convertible, and that you can still run Traefik as an accessory in front of kamal-proxy if needed.
assertions: Triggers on casual phrasing
Explains the traefik config is no longer valid and is replaced by proxy / kamal-proxy
Mentions the app_port default changed from 3000 to 80
Notes the healthcheck section was removed
Mentions Traefik can still be run as an accessory in front of kamal-proxy

evals.prompt: How do I upgrade Kamal to 2.0 without taking my site down? I run the app on three web servers.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend a rolling upgrade with kamal upgrade --rolling, explaining it follows the same steps as a normal upgrade but host by host. Should mention the alternative of running host by host with kamal upgrade -h 127.0.0.1[,127.0.0.2]. Should mention using the pre-proxy-reboot and post-proxy-reboot hooks to remove each server from upstream load balancers so no requests are dropped. Should note the upgrade must be run separately for each destination.
assertions: Recommends kamal upgrade --rolling for zero-downtime, host by host
Mentions the host-by-host alternative with kamal upgrade -h
Mentions the pre-proxy-reboot and post-proxy-reboot hooks for load balancer removal
Notes upgrade is run separately per destination

evals.prompt: We upgraded to Kamal 2 and something broke in production. How do we roll back to Kamal 1?
evals.expected_output: Should give the downgrade steps: uninstall Kamal 2.0, confirm you are running Kamal 1.9 with kamal version, then run kamal downgrade, which has the same options as kamal upgrade and reverses the process. Should note that this is why upgrading to Kamal 1.9 first matters (1.9 supports downgrading), and that the upgrade and downgrade commands can be re-run against servers that have already been upgraded or downgraded.
assertions: Lists the downgrade steps: uninstall 2.0, confirm 1.9 with kamal version, run kamal downgrade
States kamal downgrade has the same options as kamal upgrade and reverses the process
Connects downgrade support to having upgraded to Kamal 1.9 first
Notes upgrade/downgrade commands can be re-run against already-processed servers

evals.prompt: We used to generate our env vars with kamal envify. How are secrets supposed to work in Kamal 2?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that secrets moved from .env/.env.rb to .kamal/secrets, and that with destinations they are read from .kamal/secrets.<DESTINATION> first or .kamal/secrets-common if not found. Should state that kamal envify and kamal env have been removed and secrets no longer have a separate lifecycle, and that you now use dotenv command and variable substitution (e.g. $SECRET_FROM_ENV or $(op read ...)), evaluated on demand. Should note that .env and .kamal/secrets values are no longer loaded into the environment, so ERB in deploy.yml that reads ENV[...] must manually load .env (require "dotenv"; Dotenv.load(".env")).
assertions: States secrets moved from .env/.env.rb to .kamal/secrets
Mentions destination resolution: .kamal/secrets.<DESTINATION> then .kamal/secrets-common
States kamal envify and kamal env were removed
Describes dotenv command/variable substitution as the replacement
Notes .env is no longer auto-loaded, so deploy.yml ERB must load it manually

evals.prompt: can I keep using traefik on kamal 2? I've got custom routing I don't want to lose.
evals.expected_output: Should confirm Kamal 2 requires kamal-proxy but Traefik can still be used by running it as a Kamal accessory in front of kamal-proxy, with requests flowing Traefik -> kamal-proxy -> app. Should describe setting kamal-proxy's boot config via a pre-deploy hook using kamal proxy boot_config set so it doesn't publish ports on the host and adds the Traefik labels, and adding Traefik as an accessory in config/deploy.yml bound to the host port. Should mention that to switch a host already running kamal-proxy you run kamal proxy boot_config set, then kamal proxy reboot, then kamal accessory boot traefik.
assertions: Confirms Traefik can run as a Kamal accessory in front of kamal-proxy
Describes the request flow Traefik -> kamal-proxy -> app
Mentions setting kamal-proxy boot config via a pre-deploy hook with kamal proxy boot_config set
Mentions adding Traefik as an accessory in config/deploy.yml
Gives the steps to switch a host already running kamal-proxy (boot_config set, proxy reboot, accessory boot traefik)

evals.prompt: What exactly does `kamal upgrade` do to my servers, and do I need to run it once per destination?
evals.expected_output: Should list the kamal upgrade sequence: 1) stop and remove the Traefik proxy, 2) create a kamal Docker network if one doesn't exist, 3) start a kamal-proxy container in the new network, 4) reboot the currently deployed version of the app container in the new network, 5) tell kamal-proxy to send traffic to it, 6) reboot all accessories in the new network. Should confirm the command must be run separately for each destination, and should recommend testing in a non-production environment first.
assertions: Lists stopping and removing the Traefik proxy as the first step
Mentions creating the kamal Docker network if one doesn't exist
Mentions starting a kamal-proxy container and rebooting the app container in the new network
Mentions rebooting accessories in the new network
Confirms the command is run separately for each destination
Recommends testing in a non-production environment first
